rows.notes: Description legible as 'Medenham pro Biennio'. The three vertical ruling lines at the right edge are clear and the amount aligns precisely between them as 13 | 00 | 08. All currency parts fall in valid ranges. Minor uncertainty only in the spelling of the place name (handwritten 'Medenham') but amount digits are crisp.
rows.english_description: Rent from the Medenham (Medmenham) estate covering two years.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent from the Medenham estate for two years, amounting to £13 0s 8d.

rows.description: Backgate Newman pro Anno & 4ta part an.
rows.notes: Amount columns are well aligned and read as 05 | 00 | 00. Description has moderate handwriting ambiguity: first word 'Backgate' and surname 'Newman' are reasonably clear but the Latin phrase and the trailing abbreviation (appears as '4ta part an.' or similar) are uncertain. Chose this transcription based on letter shapes and common ledger phrasing.
rows.english_description: Rent from Newman of Backgate covering one year and a quarter (one year and a fourth part).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent from Newman of Backgate for one year and a quarter, amounting to £5 0s 0d.

rows.description: Mason fd Febr. D. J. B. 1742
rows.notes: Amount read as 09 | 01 | 03¾ — the small '3' pence and a clear '¾' (three farthings) symbol present. Column alignment used to separate pounds/shillings/pence. Description text is moderately ambiguous: I read 'Mason' and a date '1742'; the abbreviated middle phrase (appears 'fd Febr. D. J. B.' in the script) is uncertain but likely an abbreviated note or reference. Chose this wording to capture visible characters.
rows.english_description: Payment to a mason for work done in February (account reference D. J. B., 1742).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the mason for work in February 1742 (account D. J. B.), amounting to £9 1s 3.75d.

rows.notes: Description reads clearly as a Kidlington entry for 'Capons & Brawn xmas 1744'. Amount aligns to columns and reads 02 | 15 | 00. Spelling of 'Brawn' is inferred from handwriting but is legible in context; monetary digits are crisp and within valid ranges.
rows.english_description: Capons and brawn delivered from the Kidlington estate at Christmas 1744 (payment in kind).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Capons and brawn from Kidlington at Christmas 1744, valued at £2 15s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 10 | 00 | 00 is clear and well aligned. Description is harder to read: handwritten name looks like 'Menhinett' (or possibly 'Menshinett'/'Merhinett') followed by 'ad Mie' (the second word is uncertain). Chose a close visual reading but flagged moderate uncertainty in the transcription of the name/phrase.
rows.english_description: Receipt from Menhinett paid at a feast-day or specified term (exact feast unclear).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt from Menhinett (term unclear), amounting to £10 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Description 'Bampton pro Bienn' is legible and typical ledger phrasing. Amount columns read 02 | 00 | 00 cleanly against the ruling lines. Minor abbreviation at line end ('Bienn') is standard for 'Biennio' and readable.
rows.english_description: Rent from the Bampton estate covering two years.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent from Bampton for two years, amounting to £2 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 04 | 00 | 00 is clear. The description appears to be 'Kidlington' followed by an abbreviation that I read as 'Vic' or 'Vie' then 'pro Anno' — the middle word is somewhat ambiguous (could be 'Vic' for vicar or variant). Transcription chosen to reflect visual letters but flagged moderate uncertainty.
rows.english_description: Annual vicarage payment or vicarial receipt from Kidlington for the year.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual vicarage payment from Kidlington, amounting to £4 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount reads 00 | 05 | 00 (five shillings) and aligns well with column lines. Description looks like 'Marshall's Capons pro Biennio' but the initial name could be 'Marshall' or 'Marshall's' with a possessive mark; handwriting for the name is slightly faint. Chose this reading as the most consistent with visible letters and ledger context.
rows.english_description: Capons provided by Marshall as payment covering two years.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Capons from Marshall for two years, valued at £0 5s 0d.
